Game Drama Score.details: Decided by 21 points with 6 lead changes and 4 ties. The game saw 1 major win probability swing, and the losing team's chances peaked at 47%.

Most Valuable Play.details: Q2, 5:41, Wembanyama (SAS): Wembanyama REBOUND (Off:1 Def:1). It swung the win probability by +11.0 points.

Most Damaging Play.details: Q2, 8:41, Kornet (SAS): Kornet 3' Putback Layup (4 PTS). It swung the win probability by -9.1 points.

Clutch-Time Scoring Summary.value: Scoring over the final five minutes: home team 13, away team 12.

recap.Biggest Turning Point: The game turned at Q1, 6:09: Castle 25' 3PT Jump Shot (5 PTS) (Harper 1 AST). That single play moved SAS's chance of winning from 68% to 76%. No other moment shifted the game more, and it went SAS's way.
recap.Player Impact: No one influenced the outcome more than Wembanyama of SAS. Across 54 plays, his actions moved the win probability by a combined 55 percentage points, the most of any player on the floor.
recap.Comeback Reality: The tensest moment for a comeback came at Q1, 6:29, with OKC down 5 points. The model put their chance of pulling it off at 32%. In other words: possible, but not nothing.
recap.Hidden Momentum: The strongest run that didn't fully show on the scoreboard came at Q1, 5:40, with the game tilting SAS's way. The play in the middle of it: Vassell 25' 3PT Running Jump Shot (3 PTS) (Castle 2 AST).
recap.Model Note: These numbers come from ClutchCast's champion model, the winner of a competition between six models. The winner is judged on how honest its probabilities are on games it never trained on, not just how often it picks the right winner.
